question_text: A material has a density of 800 kg/m³. What can be concluded about this material when placed in water, which has a density of 1000 kg/m³?

options.label: A
options.text: The material will sink because it is heavier than water

options.label: B
options.text: The material will float because its density is less than the density of water

options.label: C
options.text: The material will float because it is less dense than air

options.label: D
options.text: The material will sink because density is independent of buoyancy

correct_answer: The material will float because its density is less than the density of water
explanation: Objects float when their density is less than the density of the fluid they are placed in. Since the material has a density of 800 kg/m³ and water has a density of 1000 kg/m³, the material is less dense than water and will float on the surface.
